feature: Inventory and dependency graph: metrics ingestion / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on metrics ingestion cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for metrics ingestion: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover metrics ingestion, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for metrics ingestion.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for metrics ingestion.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for OpenTelemetry Ingestion metrics ingestion inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: metrics-ingestion_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for metrics ingestion cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for metrics ingestion; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for metrics ingestion.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for metrics ingestion, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for metrics ingestion.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for metrics ingestion.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing metrics ingestion data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling metrics ingestion inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for metrics ingestion.
acceptance_criteria: Given metrics ingestion data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: metrics ingestion connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for event ingestion: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for event ingestion disaster-recovery posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for event ingestion.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for event ingestion.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for OpenTelemetry Ingestion event ingestion automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: event-ingestion_disaster-recovery_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for event ingestion disaster-recovery that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for event ingestion; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for event ingestion.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for event ingestion recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for event ingestion.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for event ingestion.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing event ingestion data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling event ingestion automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for event ingestion.
acceptance_criteria: Given event ingestion data exists, when the disaster-recovery automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: event ingestion connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for PII redaction: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Protect PII redaction resilience behavior with unit, integration, fixture, and UI tests.
backend_scope: Add fixtures, fake clients, unit tests, and API contract tests for PII redaction.
frontend_scope: Add component and e2e tests for PII redaction critical flows.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for OpenTelemetry Ingestion PII redaction tests with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: pii-redaction_resilience_tests evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for PII redaction resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for PII redaction; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for PII redaction.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for PII redaction recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for PII redaction.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for PII redaction.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing PII redaction data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling PII redaction tests broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for PII redaction.
acceptance_criteria: Given PII redaction data exists, when the resilience tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: PII redaction connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for PII redaction: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Detect PII redaction symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect disaster-recovery.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for PII redaction.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for PII redaction.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for OpenTelemetry Ingestion PII redaction health with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: pii-redaction_disaster-recovery_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for PII redaction disaster-recovery that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for PII redaction; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for PII redaction.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for PII redaction recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for PII redaction.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for PII redaction.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing PII redaction data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling PII redaction health broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for PII redaction.
acceptance_criteria: Given PII redaction data exists, when the disaster-recovery health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: PII redaction connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for trace to log correlation: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Let an agent investigate trace to log correlation resilience issues with bounded tool use, read-only diagnostics first, explicit uncertainty, and approval-gated mutations.
backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for trace to log correlation.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for trace to log correlation.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for OpenTelemetry Ingestion trace to log correlation agentic-investigation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: trace-to-log-correlation_resilience_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for trace to log correlation resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for trace to log correlation; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for trace to log correlation.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for trace to log correlation recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for trace to log correlation.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for trace to log correlation.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing trace to log correlation data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling trace to log correlation agentic-investigation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for trace to log correlation.
acceptance_criteria: Given trace to log correlation data exists, when the resilience agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: trace to log correlation connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for hot and cold telemetry: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for hot and cold telemetry scalability posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for hot and cold telemetry.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for hot and cold telemetry.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for OpenTelemetry Ingestion hot and cold telemetry automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: hot-and-cold-telemetry_scalability_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for hot and cold telemetry scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for hot and cold telemetry; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for hot and cold telemetry.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for hot and cold telemetry recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for hot and cold telemetry.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for hot and cold telemetry.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing hot and cold telemetry data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling hot and cold telemetry automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for hot and cold telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: Given hot and cold telemetry data exists, when the scalability automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: hot and cold telemetry connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
